title: Concurrent comments on same issue: storage retries once then gives up
body:
# Symptom

v3's per-ref design correctly avoids races on DIFFERENT issues
(5 concurrent `jjf new` all succeed). But N concurrent
`jjf comment` on the SAME issue fall back to one storage-layer
retry, then give up — 10 concurrent comments → ~2 land, ~8 lose
with `"concurrent write conflict ... retried once and still
raced. Retry your command."`.

# Repro

```
TARGET=<some-issue-id>
for i in $(seq 1 10); do
  (echo "comment $i" | jjf comment $TARGET -F -) &
done
wait
# Most exit 1 with the conflict message; only ~2 actually land.
```

# Risk profile

Per-issue contention is realistic in multi-agent automation:
subagents posting status updates to the same epic, multiple
clients reacting to a closed dep, dashboard auto-pollers. The
"retry your command" guidance pushes a retry loop onto every
caller.

# Fix candidates

1. Exponential backoff with bounded retries inside the storage
   layer (e.g. 3 retries with 10/40/160ms jitter). Most realistic
   contention windows are short; this absorbs them silently.
2. Surface a retry-policy knob (`JJF_RETRIES=N` env var or
   `--retries N` flag) for callers that want different defaults.
3. Promote the `concurrent_write` error to a documented retry
   contract so agent runtimes can wrap consistently.

(1) is probably enough — most callers don't need to think about
the retry policy.

# Confidence

Reproducible 100% with 10 concurrent comments. Tested in
`scratchpad/qa/r05-race/`.
